name: Neighborworks Umpqua
description_short: Non-profit community development organization providing free housing counseling, financial literacy, and affordable rental assistance across Umpqua, Oregon.
description_long:
NeighborWorks Umpqua is a non-profit organization operating in Douglas County, Oregon, with offices in Roseburg and North Bend. The organization is part of the national NeighborWorks network and focuses on community development through housing, finance, and social services.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free programs including foreclosure intervention counseling, financial literacy education, matched savings programs (IDA/Dreamsavers), affordable rental housing, and resident services. They recently added Health-Related Social Needs (HRSN) support for Oregon Health Plan members. All programs are delivered free of charge to eligible residents and community members.

NeighborWorks Umpqua distinguishes itself through dual-office coverage of a rural region, bilingual services (Spanish language support), partnerships with local agencies and health providers, and wrap-around services that connect housing with financial coaching and community support. They serve both prospective homebuyers and current residents of their properties.

As a non-profit, the organization is genuinely mission-driven and free, making it ideal for low-to-moderate income households. However, service availability is geographically limited to the Umpqua area, and they explicitly note service restrictions apply to certain programs. The organization appears well-established and legitimate, operating as an equal opportunity provider with transparent contact information and formal office locations.
pros: Completely free services with no upfront costs or hidden fees
Bilingual support available (Se Habla Español) at both locations
Comprehensive matched savings programs (IDA/Dreamsavers) that help build emergency funds
Free foreclosure intervention counseling for homeowners in crisis
Eviction prevention counseling specifically available for residents
Connected to local service providers and Oregon Health Plan benefits
Two physical office locations for in-person access in Roseburg and North Bend
cons: Service availability limited to Umpqua region (Douglas County); not accessible to most consumers
Website lacks detailed program eligibility requirements and income thresholds
Limited online application or remote service options mentioned
No information about average wait times or counseling availability
Resident Services program appears only available to current tenants of their properties, not general public
best_for: Low-to-moderate income homeowners facing foreclosure in Douglas County, Oregon
Current and prospective residents of NeighborWorks Umpqua affordable rental properties
Unbanked or underbanked individuals seeking matched savings and financial literacy programs
Oregon Health Plan members seeking non-medical support services in the Umpqua area
diagnosis: NeighborWorks Umpqua is ideal for Douglas County, Oregon residents (especially around Roseburg and North Bend) seeking free housing counseling, foreclosure help, or financial coaching. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization only serves a rural region of Oregon and is inaccessible to most consumers outside this area.
services: Affordable rental housing (property management and resident support)
Health-Related Social Needs (HRSN) support for OHP members
Resident services (social events, peer support, maintenance coordination)
Connections to local agency programs and benefits assistance
Rental Café account assistance and after-hours emergency support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
